title: union member with extra bigint prop throws at JSON.stringify
description: Input `{b: 123, c: 123n}` matches the `{b: number}` arm; mion preserves the structural extra `c: 123n` (no implicit strip). JSON.stringify then throws on the bigint. Contract: extras pass through unchanged — pre-strip them if they may carry non-serializable values.
notes: jsonStringifyThrows applies to the unsafe (mutate/preserve) path only — the matched member transforms its declared `b`, the bigint extra survives into JSON.stringify and throws. The safe (clone/direct) path strips the extra pre-serialise, so getTestDataForStringify expects a clean declared-only {b: 123} round-trip.
